skill.name: longbridge-portfolio
skill.description: Analyze Longbridge account assets, positions, profit and loss, cash flows, statements, margin data, orders, and DCA plans. The skill provides portfolio diagnosis, rebalancing, allocation, risk, attribution, and tax-loss harvesting workflows with explicit confirmation for account mutations.
skill.summary: Analyze Longbridge portfolios, account data, orders, DCA plans, and risk workflows.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external-command and weak-crypto patterns, but manual review shows those alerts are Markdown CLI documentation and false-positive text matches. No prompt injection, secret access, exfiltration code, or executable malware was found. The remaining risk is legitimate but sensitive: the skill can guide authenticated Longbridge order and DCA operations that affect real brokerage accounts.

medium_findings.title: Mutating Brokerage Commands Require Explicit Confirmation
medium_findings.description: The skill intentionally documents Longbridge CLI commands for order placement, cancellation, replacement, and DCA plan changes. These are legitimate capabilities, but mistakes can place trades or create recurring investments that commit real money.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation directly names buy, sell, cancel, replace, and DCA mutation commands and warns they affect brokerage state. The same files also require preview and explicit confirmation, which reduces but does not remove the financial risk.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown CLI Documentation
low_findings.description: The many Ruby backtick and external-command alerts are false positives from Markdown inline code and fenced examples. No executable Ruby, JavaScript, shell wrapper, or installer script was found in the reviewed skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are Markdown command names, help text, or fenced bash examples rather than executable skill code. The repository file list contains only SKILL.md and reference Markdown files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code in the reviewed context. Flagged lines discuss cash flow fields, DCA preview steps, portfolio metrics, or the YAML description rather than MD5, SHA1, or encryption routines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found portfolio and trading documentation at the flagged lines, not cryptographic operations. The confidence is high because there are no source files implementing crypto in the skill package.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Is Documentation Link
low_findings.description: The single network finding is a GitHub link to longbridge-terminal installation guidance inside an error-handling table. It is not an outbound request, webhook, or exfiltration endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a Markdown hyperlink used when the CLI is missing. No code performs a network call to this URL.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI Commands Operate On Brokerage Account Data
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs agents to run Longbridge CLI commands to fetch account data and, for some workflows, to submit account mutations after confirmation. This is expected for the skill but requires user authentication, careful consent handling, and avoidance of silent retries.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The CLI workflow is explicit in the skill instructions and includes real account operations. The risk is operational rather than covert because the documentation repeatedly mandates confirmation.

content.value_statement: Longbridge users need one place to inspect account data, portfolio risk, and order workflows. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Longbridge CLI tasks with confirmation steps for sensitive actions.
actual_capabilities: Retrieve Longbridge account assets, cash flow records, statements, and bank card summaries.
Review stock and fund positions across authenticated Longbridge sub-accounts.
Analyze profit and loss, allocation drift, concentration, sector exposure, and portfolio risk.
Estimate margin requirements and maximum buy or sell quantities for supported symbols.
Guide order list, detail, buy, sell, cancel, and replace workflows with preview and confirmation.
Manage DCA plan review, creation, pause, resume, update, and stop workflows with strict confirmation.
limitations: Most account and trading workflows require Longbridge authentication and the correct permission scope.
Mutating order and DCA actions must not run until the user gives explicit confirmation.
Portfolio and risk metrics depend on available Longbridge data and recent price history.
Analysis is informational and is not investment, tax, legal, or financial advice.

use_cases.title: Check Portfolio Health
use_cases.description: Review holdings, concentration, sector exposure, currency exposure, and risk metrics from Longbridge account data.

use_cases.title: Prepare A Rebalance Plan
use_cases.description: Compare current weights with target weights and produce a trade plan with drift, cost, and tax considerations.

use_cases.title: Control Orders And DCA Plans
use_cases.description: Preview order or recurring investment changes, confirm every detail, and execute only after explicit approval.

prompt_templates.title: Portfolio Snapshot
prompt_templates.prompt: Show my Longbridge portfolio summary, including assets, cash, positions, and today's profit and loss.

prompt_templates.title: Risk Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my Longbridge holdings for concentration, volatility, VaR, CVaR, drawdown, and major risk contributors.

prompt_templates.title: Rebalance Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare my current Longbridge portfolio with a target allocation and draft a rebalance plan without placing orders.

prompt_templates.title: Confirmed DCA Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Preview a new monthly DCA plan for the symbol and amount I provide, then wait for my explicit confirmation before execution.

output: Portfolio risk report with concentration, sector exposure, currency exposure, volatility, VaR, and CVaR.
A short summary of the largest risk contributors and data gaps.
A reminder that historical risk estimates do not predict future losses.

output_examples.input: Create a weekly DCA plan for a stock.
output: A preview listing symbol, amount, currency, frequency, day, start date, and end condition.
A clear warning that the plan can place future buy orders from the brokerage account.
A request for explicit confirmation before any mutating command runs.

output_examples.input: Plan a rebalance toward my target weights.
output: A comparison of current and target weights with drift by holding.
A proposed trade list marked as informational until the user chooses to place orders.
Notes about transaction costs, tax impact, and position feasibility.

best_practices: Use JSON output from Longbridge commands when available so results are easier to inspect.
Preview every order or DCA mutation and require explicit confirmation before execution.
State data limitations and keep analysis separate from personal investment advice.
anti_patterns: Do not place, cancel, replace, or retry orders based on vague user approval.
Do not guess missing DCA amount, currency, frequency, start date, or end condition.
Do not present model calculations as guaranteed returns, tax advice, or financial advice.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Longbridge account?
faq.answer: Yes, most account, position, statement, order, and DCA workflows require Longbridge authentication.

faq.question: Can it place real trades?
faq.answer: It can guide order workflows when authenticated, but it must preview the action and wait for explicit confirmation.

faq.question: Can it manage DCA recurring investments?
faq.answer: Yes, it covers DCA list, create, update, pause, resume, and stop workflows with strict confirmation rules.

faq.question: Does it provide investment advice?
faq.answer: No. It provides informational analysis and workflow guidance based on Longbridge data.

faq.question: What happens if the Longbridge CLI is not installed?
faq.answer: The skill tells the user to install longbridge-terminal or use an available MCP fallback when appropriate.

faq.question: Which languages does it support?
faq.answer: The skill instructs responses to match the user in Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, or English.
